figures_detected.description: N-CVT C/U connector diagram showing terminal 22 with voltmeter connected between terminal 22 and ground
figures_detected.associated_step: Step B - Check harness voltage between terminal 22 and ground

nodes.text:
Check input signal
1. Turn ignition switch "ON"
2. Check voltage between N-CVT control unit connector terminal (20) and ground
Voltage: Battery voltage

Check following items:
● Fuse # 12
● N-CVT warning lamp bulb
● N-CVT relay (refer to electrical component inspection)
● Harness continuity between ignition switch and N-CVT control unit

Check engine speed signal
1. Start engine
2. ● Select "DATA MONITOR"
● Read ENGINE RPM from monitor
● Check engine speed in comparison with throttle opening
— OR —
Check harness voltage between terminal 22 and ground.
Voltage: 0.9 - 4.5 V

Check following items:
● Harness continuity between N-CVT control unit terminal (22) and engine control unit terminal (2)
● Perform Self-diagnosis of engine control unit (refer to EF & EC)
Perform Self diagnosis after test drive

1. Check the N-CVT input and output signals
2. Re-check N-CVT control unit pin terminals for damage and correct connection of control unit harness connector
